skill.name: qryma-search
skill.description: Search the web with multiple output formats using the Qryma API. Use this skill when the user wants to search the web, find information on a specific topic, says "search", "look up", "find information", "web search", or needs quick answers from the internet. Supports Markdown format for readability, JSON for structured data, and Brave search-like format.
skill.summary: Web search skill with Markdown, JSON, and Brave-style output formats using the Qryma API.
skill.author: qryma-ai

security_audit.summary: All 112 static analyzer findings were evaluated and determined to be FALSE POSITIVES. The env_access patterns are legitimate API key configuration. Network calls go to the documented Qryma API endpoint. External command findings are markdown documentation examples, not actual shell execution. Filesystem access targets standard configuration directories. No malicious patterns, code execution, data exfiltration, or obfuscation detected. The skill is safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Configuration File Access
low_findings.description: The skill reads API configuration from ~/.qryma/.env and .env files. This is standard practice for API key management but involves filesystem access.

content.user_title: Search the Web with Qryma API
content.value_statement: Quickly find accurate information from the web without leaving your AI assistant. Get search results in multiple formats optimized for AI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Execute web searches using the Qryma search API with customizable parameters
Return results in Markdown format for readable display
Return results in JSON format for structured data processing
Return results in Brave search-like format for compatibility
Support pagination with start offset parameter
Filter results by language and safe search settings
limitations: Requires a valid Qryma API key for authentication
Limited to Qryma search engine index, not all web content
Cannot access paywalled or private content
Results depend on Qryma API availability and rate limits

use_cases.title: Research and Fact-Checking
use_cases.description: Quickly verify facts, find sources, and gather information for research projects. Use Markdown format for readable summaries.

use_cases.title: Development and Technical Research
use_cases.description: Find documentation, tutorials, and solutions to technical problems. Use JSON format for integrating results into workflows.

use_cases.title: Market Intelligence
use_cases.description: Gather competitive information, track industry trends, and monitor brand mentions. Use detailed results for comprehensive analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "how to learn Python programming" and return the top 5 results in Markdown format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple search with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Format-Specific Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "latest AI trends 2024" and return results as JSON for data processing.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "machine learning best practices" with 10 results, English language, and detailed output enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Paginated Deep Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for "cloud security frameworks" starting from result 10, with 15 results maximum, safe search enabled.

output_examples.input: Search for "Python tutorial for beginners" with Markdown format
output_examples.output:
1. Python.org Official Tutorial
   https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/
   - The official Python tutorial covering basic concepts and syntax

2. Real Python - Python 3 Tutorials
   https://realpython.com/start-here/
   - Comprehensive Python tutorials for all skill levels

3. W3Schools Python Tutorial
   https://www.w3schools.com/python/
   - Interactive Python lessons with examples and exercises

output_examples.input: Search for "REST API design patterns" with JSON format

best_practices: Start with basic searches and refine parameters based on result quality
Use Markdown format for human-readable results and JSON for programmatic processing
Configure your API key in environment variables or .env file for secure storage
anti_patterns: Do not make excessive requests in rapid succession - respect rate limits
Avoid using the skill without configuring API key - it will fail at runtime
Do not rely on search results for critical decisions without verification

faq.question: How do I get a Qryma API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Qryma website and sign up for an account. You will receive an API key that can be used with this skill. Check their pricing page for free tier limits.

faq.question: Where should I store my API key?
faq.answer: Store your API key in the QRYMA_API_KEY environment variable, or create a .env file in your project directory with the key. You can also create ~/.qryma/.env for global configuration.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Three formats are supported: raw (JSON), md (Markdown), and brave (Brave search-like JSON). Use the --format flag to specify your preferred format.

faq.question: Can I customize the number of results?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --max-results parameter to specify how many results to return. The default is 5, but you can increase this based on your needs and API limits.

faq.question: Does this skill support different languages?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --lang parameter to specify the language code. The default is English (en). Refer to the Qryma documentation for supported language codes.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is fully compatible with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. It can be installed and used within any supported AI assistant environment.
